title: The EBA publishes Decision harmonising reporting of SEPA data by national authorities
clean_title: The EBA publishes Decision harmonising reporting of SEPA data by national authorities
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) today published a Decision harmonising how National Competent Authorities (NCAs) report under the SEPA Regulation. The Decision complements the existing European Commission’s Implementing Regulation which requires all Payment Service Providers (PSPs) to report data on charges for credit transfers and payment accounts, as well as the shares of transactions rejected due to EU sanctions. The Decision streamlines the second step of this reporting process – from the NCAs to the EBA and the European Commission.
why_selected: Thématique réglementation ; source jugée fiable (european banking authority) ; impact réglementaire.
impacts: Peut modifier la conformité, les reportings ou la gouvernance des acteurs financiers.
title_signature: authorities data decision eba harmonising national publishes reporting sepa
normalized_title: the eba publishes decision harmonising reporting of sepa data by national authorities

title: The EBA consults on major simplification of supervisory reporting to deliver a simpler, smarter and more proportionate framework
clean_title: The EBA consults on major simplification of supervisory reporting to deliver a simpler, smarter and more proportionate framework
summary: The European Banking Authority (EBA) is announcing a series of measures to significantly simplify EU supervisory reporting. A public consultation on revised Implementing Technical Standards (ITS) on supervisory reporting and on supervisory benchmarking reporting is open until 10 July 2026, and until 10 May 2026 for IFRS 18-related requirements.
